name: A1.1
headline: Absolute Beginner
summary: Learners start from zero. They learn the alphabet, pronunciation, basic greetings, and simple self-introduction (name, age, country).

name: A1.2
headline: Basic Communication
summary: Learners begin to handle simple everyday situations. They can talk about family, work, and daily routines using short and simple sentences.

name: A1.3
headline: Foundation Completion
summary: Learners strengthen their basics and can manage simple interactions such as ordering food, shopping, and asking for directions.

name: A2.1
headline: Expanding Basics
summary: Learners can describe daily life, people, and places. They start expressing needs and opinions more clearly.

name: A2.2
headline: Improving Fluency
summary: Learners gain more confidence in speaking. They can participate in simple conversations and talk about past events in a basic way.

name: A2.3
headline: Pre-Intermediate Transition
summary: Learners understand longer conversations and express themselves more comfortably in everyday situations.

name: B1.1
headline: Independent User Start
summary: Learners can communicate in a wider range of topics such as work, travel, and education. They begin to describe experiences and tell stories.

name: B1.2
headline: Confidence Building
summary: Learners can express opinions clearly, engage in discussions, and handle most everyday situations independently.

name: B1.3
headline: Exam Preparation Level
summary: Learners are close to the B1 exam level. They can understand longer texts and communicate with reasonable fluency.

name: B2.1
headline: Advanced Communication
summary: Learners can discuss more complex topics, express detailed opinions, and understand more advanced content.

name: B2.2
headline: Fluency & Precision
summary: Learners communicate fluently and naturally. They can handle in-depth discussions and use the language with a high level of accuracy.
